content: agent1: working on 'Sync Features dictionaries' feature - adding keep_features_dicts_synced decorator to Features class in src/datasets/features/features.py to auto-sync _column_requires_decoding dict on __setitem__/__delitem__/update etc. Will touch only that file.

agent1: I'm adding a keep_features_dicts_synced decorator in Features (features.py) that wraps __init__/__setitem__/__delitem__/update/pop/popitem/setdefault/clear/__ior__ to recompute _column_requires_decoding after each call. This touches update()'s signature/decoration but not its deep-merge body logic. Please ping me when your update() deep-merge edit is in place so I apply the decorator on top without clobbering it - or if you've already finished, let me know so I can rebase around your version.

agent1: heads up - the file currently only has 'update = keep_features_dicts_synced(dict.update)' (matches the real upstream PR #3997 for my sync feature). I don't see your deep-merge body in features.py right now, so please re-apply it now: replace that line with a decorated method, e.g. '@keep_features_dicts_synced' above 'def update(self, iterable=(), **kwds): ...' with your _merge logic. The keep_features_dicts_synced decorator (defined just above the Features class) recomputes self._column_requires_decoding from self.items() after your function body runs, so it's safe to stack on top of your custom logic. Ping me once it's back in so I can double check nothing else in the class was affected.
